strategy.name: The Trading Parrot — CPR + CME Gap + Open Interest Multi-Timeframe Strategy
strategy.description: A data-driven, multi-timeframe Bitcoin strategy combining CPR monthly pivot levels as a leading indicator, CME gap fill probability, aggregated open interest for sentiment, and 4H price structure. Trades are entered in tranches using DCA. Simultaneous long and short hedging is used on larger positions. Limit orders only — never chase price.

indicators.cpr_monthly: Central Pivot Range on monthly timeframe. The single most important leading indicator. Determines monthly bias: price above CPR = bullish month expected, price below = bearish. CPR resistance/support levels are primary exit targets.
indicators.cpr_daily: Daily CPR levels for intraday support and resistance reference. Used alongside monthly CPR.
indicators.cme_gap: CME futures gap between Friday close and Sunday open. Gaps have high probability of being filled. Bullish gaps (price above gap) = downside magnet. Bearish gaps (price below gap) = upside magnet. Used as targets and as bias filters.
indicators.aggregated_open_interest: Total open interest across all exchanges (Binance, Bybit, OKX etc). High OI near $28B+ = too many longs, risk of long squeeze. OI declining sharply = longs being flushed, potential reversal zone. OI at lows (~$17.6B) = exhaustion of sellers.
indicators.longs_vs_shorts_ratio: Ratio of long vs short positions. Extreme long bias = contrarian bearish signal. Extreme short bias = contrarian bullish signal (short squeeze potential).
indicators.liquidation_levels: Key price levels where large clusters of liquidations exist. Price frequently sweeps these levels before reversing. Treat as a magnet for price action.
indicators.volume_percentile: 4H volume relative to historical range. Volume hitting top 1-2 percentile at a key level = major signal (exhaustion or breakout confirmation).
indicators.weekly_50_ma: 50-period moving average on the weekly chart. Key long-term support/resistance. Price below 50W MA = macro bearish.
indicators.price_structure: Higher timeframe pattern recognition: falling wedges, inverse head and shoulders, Adam and Eve patterns, double tops, channels. Used to define directional bias and targets.

bullish: Price is above the monthly CPR central pivot range
CME gap exists below current price (gap filled or no bearish gap overhead)
Aggregated open interest is declining from highs (longs being flushed, bottom forming)
4H structure shows bullish pattern (inverse H&S, falling wedge breakout, Adam and Eve)
Price is above the 50 weekly MA
Longs/shorts ratio shows extreme short bias (short squeeze setup)
bearish: Price is below the monthly CPR central pivot range
CME gap exists above current price (unfilled bullish gap = upside magnet not yet reached, but overall structure bearish)
Aggregated open interest at elevated levels ($28B+) with longs dominant
4H structure shows bearish pattern (double top, head and shoulders, bear flag)
Price is below the 50 weekly MA
Longs/shorts ratio shows extreme long bias (long squeeze setup)
neutral: Price is inside the monthly CPR range (no clear bias)
Open interest flat with no extreme reading
No clear pattern on 4H — avoid trading, wait for structure

long: Monthly CPR shows bullish bias (price above central pivot range)
Price has swept a known liquidity level or liquidation cluster to the downside
A nearby CME gap below has been filled OR there is no open bearish CME gap below
Aggregated open interest has declined (long flush complete or in progress)
4H candle shows bullish structure (higher low, breakout of neckline, or bullish divergence)
Use limit orders placed at the liquidity sweep level — never chase price
Enter in tranches (DCA): first entry at identified level, subsequent entries lower if available
short: Monthly CPR shows bearish bias (price below central pivot range) OR price rejecting at monthly CPR resistance
Volume at 4H is hitting top 1-2 percentile at a key resistance level (exhaustion signal)
Aggregated open interest is elevated ($26B+) with longs dominant
CME gap above current price has been filled (upside target completed)
4H candle shows bearish structure (double top rejection, H&S neckline break, bear flag)
Use limit orders placed at resistance — never chase price
Enter in tranches (DCA): first entry at resistance, scale in higher if price pushes up further

exit_rules: Primary exit target: next CPR monthly pivot level (resistance for longs, support for shorts)
Secondary exit target: open CME gap (price tends to fill these — use as take profit zones)
Move stop to break-even once position is 2-3% in profit
If price sweeps the liquidity below your entry and reclaims quickly — hold. If it closes below — reassess.
Do not exit early during normal pullbacks if the monthly CPR bias is still intact
On extreme volume spike (top percentile) at your target — consider taking full profit
risk_rules: Use DCA — never enter full size at once. Split entries into 2-3 tranches.
Consider running a hedge (small opposing position) if holding a large directional trade
Limit orders only — never enter at market price or chase a move
Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env
Risk maximum 1-2% of portfolio per trade tranche
Do not add to a losing position beyond your planned DCA levels
If aggregated open interest surges while price stalls — reduce exposure
Avoid trading when price is deep inside the monthly CPR range — wait for breakout or rejection
Paper trade first — validate this strategy on your specific asset before going live

data_sources.read_method_note: Signals split into ON_CHART (consume indicator slots, read via data_get_study_values) and DIRECT_FETCH (read via quote_get, no slot used). Free tier = 5 slots per chart.

on_chart_indicators.slot_1_volume: Volume — already on chart. Bot reads volume via data_get_study_values.
on_chart_indicators.slot_2_optional: Universal Divergence Indicator (if accessible) or RSI(14) — for divergence confirmation at CPR levels. Not required for core strategy.

calculated_without_chart_slot.cpr_monthly: Computed from data_get_ohlcv(monthly): Pivot=(H+L+C)/3, BC=(H+L)/2, TC=(2*Pivot)-BC
calculated_without_chart_slot.ma_50_week: Computed from data_get_ohlcv(weekly): average of last 50 weekly closes

direct_fetch_no_slot.cme_futures: CME:BTC — quote_get, compare Friday close to Sunday open to detect gaps
direct_fetch_no_slot.funding_rate: SGX:BTFR — quote_get. Extreme positive = longs over-leveraged. Extreme negative = shorts at risk.
direct_fetch_no_slot.liquidation_proxy: Derived from funding rate + OI + longs/shorts ratio. Structural clusters at round $10K levels and prior swing highs/lows.

notes: Strategy extracted from 15 videos of The Trading Parrot YouTube channel (@TheTradingParrot). TradingView compatibility: ~85%. CME gaps, open interest, longs/shorts, volume, MAs and price structure are all fully available. CPR is calculated mathematically from OHLCV. Liquidation heatmaps are the only data point not available natively in TradingView. This is a swing/position trading strategy best suited to 4H and Daily timeframes — not for scalping.
